# JUSTIS

JUSTIS is an open project for building a Universal Code of Justice through structured moral deliberation.

## Read first

- README.md
- PROJECT_BRIEF.en.md
- AGENTS.md
- CONTRIBUTING.md
- GOOD_FIRST_CONTRIBUTIONS.md
- OPEN_QUESTIONS.md
- DECISIONS.md
- PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md
- codex/UNIVERSAL_JUSTICE_CODE.md

## Core directories

- specifications: methodology, standards, source maps, DSL/JML.
- protocols: deliberations, ratifications, consensus status.
- research: exploratory notes on axioms and topics.
- arguments: structured argument and counterargument trees.
- codex: concise laws, presumptions, weighing rules, glossary.
- cases: moral and legal test cases.
- sources: notes on authors, traditions, and texts.
- templates: reusable document formats.

## Agent tasks

Agents are invited to:

- find contradictions;
- generate edge cases;
- propose objections;
- compare principles with ethical traditions;
- detect hidden assumptions;
- improve structure;
- translate documents;
- submit contributions through issues or pull requests.

## Do not

- present your output as final moral truth;
- call a result consensus without a protocol;
- remove unresolved objections;
- optimize for persuasion over truth;
- hide uncertainty;
- erase minority positions without argument;
- change the codex without preserving the argument trail.

## Best contribution

The strongest contribution is a well-formed objection, edge case, or structural improvement that makes the Code more truthful, less biased, and harder to abuse.
